problem_17.natural_language: A subgroup of index `2` is normal.
problem_17.lean: theorem subgroup_normal_of_index_eq_two {G : Type*} [Group G] (H : Subgroup G) (hH : H.index = 2) : H.Normal
problem_17.natural_language_proof:
Proof: since `H.index = 2`, there are exactly two left cosets of `H`: namely `H` itself and the
other coset `gH` for any `g ∉ H`. The same holds for right cosets. For any `g`, if `g ∈ H` then
`gH = H = Hg`. If `g ∉ H`, then `gH` is the unique left coset different from `H`, and `Hg` is the
unique right coset different from `H`, so necessarily `gH = Hg`. Thus `gH = Hg` for all `g`, which
is exactly normality.
